pi_items.source: Code analysis of setup_task.sh and export_result.sh confirm this directory and file path are created and monitored
Code analysis of verifier.py confirms exact layer names and verification logic matches metadata
Verifier checks: abs(cx - (-48)) < 2 and abs(cy - 0) < 2; consistent with description and task geometry
Verifier checks: abs(w - 24) < 2 and abs(h - 24) < 2; consistent across task description and verifier logic
Verifier checks: (abs(w - 600) < 10 and abs(h - 36) < 5); matches task description
Verifier checks: abs(other_end[0] - 48) < 10; task description confirms 'left edge aligns at X=48'
Verifier checks: abs(y1 - 108) < 5 and abs(y2 - 0) < 5 and abs(y3 - (-108)) < 5; spacing is consistent (72" clear = 3x trench width spacing per septic standards)
Verifier checks: dist1 = math.hypot(p1[0] - (-36), p1[1] - 0); this is the D-Box outlet (right edge of -48 center box)

pi_items.verified_value: LibreCAD environment configured as Default=4 (millimeters), NOT inches
pi_items.source: setup_librecad.sh sets [Units] Default=4; LibreCAD documentation and GitHub source code confirm 4=Millimeters, 1=Inches. Web search and code analysis confirm contradiction.

pi_items.metadata_value: Distribution Box is 24" x 24" (implied to be standard from description)
pi_items.verified_value: Standard septic system distribution boxes are typically 16-20 inches in primary dimensions; 24"x24" exceeds real-world standards
pi_items.source: Web search of septic distribution box specifications from American Concrete, Tank Depot, Jensen Infrastructure - all cite standard sizes under 24 inches. However, this is a SYNTHETIC task specification, not a claim about real equipment.

pi_items.metadata_value: Trenches 600" long x 36" wide, spaced 72" clear (3x width spacing)
pi_items.verified_value: Per septic system standards: trenches typically 1-3 feet wide, up to 100 feet long, spaced minimum 3x-width center-to-center; task specification meets or exceeds standards
pi_items.source: Web search of septic drainfield design specifications from MU Extension, InspectAPedia, Indiana health dept. Task values are realistic and compliant.

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic CAD task with internally consistent geometric specifications. Key verified facts: (1) Output path, layer names, and all coordinate values are mutually consistent across task.json, task description, and verifier.py validation logic; (2) Geometric specifications are realistic - trenches comply with septic system standards (3x-width spacing, realistic dimensions); (3) CRITICAL DISCREPANCY: Task description claims units are 'inches' but LibreCAD environment is configured to Default=4 (millimeters), creating a unit mismatch that would cause the task to fail; (4) D-Box size (24"x24") exceeds typical real-world standards (16-20 inches), but this is acceptable for a synthetic specification task.
